translation: Alif, Lam, Meem
commentary:
الٓمٓ

These disconnected letters – Alif Lam Meem – open this remarkable Surah, just as they opened Surah Al-Ankabut before it. These letters serve as a divine signature, reminding us that what follows comes from Allah ﷻ alone.

translation: The Byzantines have been defeated
commentary:
غُلِبَتِ ٱلرُّومُ

Allah ﷻ declares: "The Romans have been defeated." This is not merely historical reporting – it's divine commentary on world events as they unfolded. The word غُلِبَتِ uses the passive form in Arabic, indicating that Rome lost in battle. When a nation defeats another in war, the victor is called الغالب and the defeated is المغلوب (al-maghloub). Here, Rome is identified as the defeated party.

To understand the context, we must look at the geopolitical landscape of the early seventh century. For several hundred years, two great empires dominated the known world: the Persian Empire in the East and the Roman Empire in the West. The Persians, under the Zoroastrian dynasty that traced back to Cyrus, had expanded their territory significantly. On the other side stood Rome, which had converted to Christianity roughly three centuries after Prophet Isa (AS).

These two superpowers were locked in constant struggle over strategic territories – modern-day Turkey, Syria, and Palestine. Control would shift back and forth between them through the centuries. But from 602 to 614 CE – a period covering much of the Prophet's ﷺ early mission in Makkah – the Persians dealt the Romans a crushing blow unlike their previous conflicts. They captured Jerusalem, destroyed much of the Christian holy sites, and pushed so deep into Roman territory that even Constantinople, the great capital, nearly fell.

This was no ordinary military defeat. The Romans had suffered catastrophic losses, and the entire balance of power appeared to have shifted permanently in Persia's favor.

translation: In the nearest land. But they, after their defeat, will overcome
commentary:
فِىٓ أَدْنَى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ

"In the nearest land, and they, after their defeat, will overcome."

The phrase أَدْنَى ٱلْأَرْضِ – "the nearest land" – carries geographic and spiritual significance. The battles between Rome and Persia were happening right at Arabia's doorstep, in territories immediately adjacent to the Arabian Peninsula. The word أَدْنَى means "nearest" or "closest," from the same root as the word دَانٍ used elsewhere in the Quran to describe proximity.

This wasn't some distant conflict that Arabs could ignore. Major world events were unfolding in their neighborhood, yet they remained largely oblivious to their deeper meaning – a theme Allah ﷻ highlighted at the end of the previous Surah when He asked whether they realized that people were being "picked off" right around them.

The truly miraculous part of this ayah comes in its second half: وَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ – "and they, after their defeat, will be victorious." The Quraysh, hearing this prediction, were incredulous. The Romans had just suffered their worst defeat in memory. Jerusalem was lost, huge swaths of territory had fallen, and the empire appeared on the verge of collapse. The idea that they would somehow stage a comeback seemed laughable.

But Allah ﷻ was making a definitive prediction about future events, and the Muslims believed it completely.

translation: Within three to nine years. To Allah belongs the command before and after. And that day the believers will rejoice
commentary:
فِى بِضْعِ سِنِينَ ۗ لِلَّهِ ٱلْأَمْرُ مِن قَبْلُ وَمِنۢ بَعْدُ ۚ وَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ

"Within a few years. To Allah belongs the command before and after. And that day the believers will rejoice."

The word بِضْع in Arabic specifically refers to a number between three and nine. This linguistic precision allowed for a remarkable historical moment. Abu Bakr (RA), full of confidence in Allah's promise, approached some of the Quraysh polytheists and proposed a wager: the Romans would indeed make their comeback. Initially, he wagered ten camels over three years. But when the Prophet ﷺ informed him that bid' meant up to nine years, Abu Bakr (RA) returned and increased both the timeframe and the wager to one hundred camels.

At the time of this revelation, it was the fifth year of Prophethood. Nine years later would be the fourteenth year – and exactly nine years after this revelation was given, the Romans achieved a stunning military comeback and defeated the Persians decisively. The Quran's prophecy was fulfilled with perfect precision.

But Allah ﷻ frames this prediction with a profound theological statement: لِلَّهِ ٱلْأَمْرُ مِن قَبْلُ وَمِنۢ بَعْدُ – "To Allah belongs the command before and after." Before these empires even existed, Allah ﷻ controlled all affairs. After they crumble to dust, He will still control all affairs. These grand civilizations that seem so permanent are merely actors in a divine script.

The ayah ends with another layer of prophecy: وَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ – "And on that day the believers will rejoice." 

Why would believers rejoice at a Roman victory? The early Muslims had a natural affinity for the People of the Book. The Quraysh worshipped idols and fire, much like the Zoroastrian Persians, so they cheered for Persia's victory. The Muslims, who believed in prophets, books, and the afterlife like the Christians and Jews, naturally felt solidarity with Rome despite not yet having direct extensive dealings with Christian communities.

But there's something deeper here. Nine years from the fifth year of Prophethood brings us to the fourteenth year – and what happened in the fourteenth year? The Battle of Badr. On the very same day that the Romans achieved their historic comeback against Persia, the Muslims won their first major victory against the Quraysh at Badr. The believers' joy was not just about Rome's victory – it was about their own divinely-aided triumph. Allah ﷻ had called the victory at Badr nine years in advance, and the Companions may not have even fully realized what يَفْرَحُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ meant until they experienced that joy themselves on that blessed day.

translation: In the victory of Allah. He gives victory to whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful
commentary:
بِنَصْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ يَنصُرُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

"By the help of Allah. He helps whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful."

The believers' joy comes بِنَصْرِ ٱللَّهِ – through the help of Allah ﷻ, through His divine aid and support. This wasn't about human military strategy or superior numbers – at Badr, the Muslims were vastly outnumbered. This was about Allah's ﷻ intervention in human affairs.

يَنصُرُ مَن يَشَآءُ – "He helps whom He wills." Victory doesn't come through might alone. Allah ﷻ grants victory to whomever He chooses, according to His wisdom. The same Allah ﷻ Who granted the Romans victory over the Persians granted the Muslims victory over the Quraysh.

The ayah concludes by identifying Allah ﷻ with two of His beautiful names: ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ – the Exalted in Might, the Merciful. He is Al-Aziz, the One of ultimate authority and power, Whose will cannot be challenged. Yet He is also Ar-Rahim, always merciful, always compassionate. His help comes not from capriciousness but from His mercy toward those who turn to Him.

translation: [It is] the promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in His promise, but most of the people do not know
commentary:
وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱللَّهُ وَعْدَهُۥ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

"The promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in His promise, but most of the people do not know."

وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ – this is the promise of Allah ﷻ. Everything that was just described – the Roman comeback, the believers' joy, the victory at Badr – all of this was promised by Allah ﷻ before it happened.

لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱللَّهُ وَعْدَهُۥ – Allah ﷻ does not go back on His promise. When He says something will happen, it happens. His word is absolute and unshakeable. But here's the tragedy: وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ – most people don't have a clue. They don't understand that Allah's ﷻ promises are guarantees, not mere possibilities. They see world events as random outcomes of human ambition and military strength, never recognizing the divine hand orchestrating everything.

translation: They know what is apparent of the worldly life, but they, of the Hereafter, are unaware
commentary:
يَعْلَمُونَ ظَـٰهِرًا مِّنَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ غَـٰفِلُونَ

"They know what is apparent of the worldly life, but they, of the Hereafter, are heedless."

This ayah explains why most people don't recognize Allah's ﷻ promises. يَعْلَمُونَ ظَـٰهِرًا مِّنَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا – they know the ظَاهِر, the outward, the surface, the obvious aspects of worldly life. They understand cause and effect on a material level. They can analyze military tactics, economic trends, political maneuvering. They see the shallow, external dimensions of events.

But they miss what's happening in the غَيْب, the unseen realm. They don't perceive the spiritual realities underlying material events. And because their knowledge is limited to the visible world, وَهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ غَـٰفِلُونَ – they are completely heedless and oblivious to the afterlife. 

The word غَافِلُون means to be utterly unmindful, to be in a state of complete neglect and ignorance of something. The afterlife exists entirely in the unseen realm, so those who only see the surface of this world remain blind to the reality that awaits them.

translation: Do they not contemplate within themselves? Allah has not created the heavens and the earth and what is between them except in truth and for a specified term. And indeed, many of the people, in [the matter of] the meeting with their Lord, are disbelievers
commentary:
أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِم ۗ مَّا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۗ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِلِقَآئِ رَبِّهِمْ لَكَـٰفِرُونَ

"Do they not contemplate within themselves? Allah has not created the heavens and the earth and what is between them except in truth and for a specified term. And indeed, many of the people, in the meeting with their Lord, are disbelievers."

أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِم – didn't they engage in deep thought within themselves? This is an invitation to introspection, to contemplation, to تَفَكُّر – profound reflection. Look inward, Allah ﷻ says. Think deeply about your own existence.

مَّا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ – Allah ﷻ did not create the heavens and the earth and everything between them except بِٱلْحَقِّ, with truth, with purpose, with justice. Nothing in creation is random or meaningless. Even the great empires of Rome and Persia, even their wars and their victories and defeats – all of it has purpose in Allah's ﷻ grand design.

Perhaps, some scholars suggest, the entire conflict between these empires, the Roman defeat and comeback, all of it happened so that these few ayahs could be revealed. The ayahs are more valuable, more purposeful than the entire historical drama. The treasures of divine revelation outweigh the rise and fall of civilizations.

وَأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى – and for a specified term, a well-named deadline. Everything Allah ﷻ created has an expiration date. These heavens and this earth will not last forever. There is a final day, and it has many names: the Day of Judgment, the Day of Resurrection, the Day of Meeting, the Day of Winning and Losing. It is مُسَمًّى – clearly named, well-defined, inevitable.

وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِلِقَآئِ رَبِّهِمْ لَكَـٰفِرُونَ – yet the fact remains that a huge number of people, especially when it comes to meeting with their Master, are in complete denial. The previous Surah, Al-Ankabut, began and ended with the theme of people denying that they will meet Allah ﷻ. This Surah continues that theme. They refuse to contemplate their own mortality, refuse to prepare for the inevitable meeting with their Creator.

translation: Have they not traveled through the earth and observed how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in power, and they plowed the earth and built it up more than they have built it up, and their messengers came to them with clear evidences. And Allah would not ever have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves
commentary:
أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَأَثَارُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَعَمَرُوهَآ أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوهَا وَجَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ۖ فَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ

"Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in power, and they plowed the earth and built it up more than they have built it up, and their messengers came to them with clear evidences. And Allah would not ever have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves."

أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ – haven't they traveled through the earth? Allah ﷻ shifts from internal contemplation to external observation. Look around you. Explore the lands.

فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ – and see what became of those who came before them. What was their ultimate outcome? The word عَاقِبَة ('aqibah) means final result, ultimate consequence.

كَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً – they were far more powerful, far more intense in their strength than these people today. The Quraysh had no grand civilization, no monuments, no advanced technology. But previous nations had built great empires.

وَأَثَارُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْضَ – they tilled the land. The word أَثَارُوا comes from the root meaning to lift something up, to turn over soil, to cultivate. They were agricultural societies that made full use of their land. Before the age of factories and offices, agriculture was the foundation of economic power. Fertile, productive land was what made a nation wealthy and worth conquering.

وَعَمَرُوهَآ أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوهَا – and they built upon it, gave life to it, created civilizations far more advanced than what you have built. The word عَمَرُوا means to build, to construct, to bring life to the land. It's related to عِمَارَة, tall buildings and grand structures. Where are your towers, Quraysh? Where are your monuments? Previous nations built things that dwarf anything you've accomplished.

وَجَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ – and their messengers came to them with the clearest proofs, with self-evident truths from Allah ﷻ. They had divine guidance. They had prophets who showed them the straight path.

فَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ – so Allah ﷻ was never going to wrong them. It would never be befitting of Allah ﷻ to do injustice to them. They had everything they needed: strength, resources, knowledge, and divine guidance.

وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ – but they used to wrong their own selves. They destroyed themselves through their own choices. Their downfall wasn't Allah's ﷻ doing – it was their own. When you reject clear guidance, when you oppress others, when you live in arrogance despite having been given everything, you are setting yourself up for destruction.

translation: Then the end of those who did evil was the worst [consequence] because they denied the signs of Allah and used to ridicule them
commentary:
ثُمَّ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَـٰٓـُٔوا۟ ٱلسُّوٓأَىٰٓ أَن كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَكَانُوا۟ بِهَا يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ

"Then the end of those who did evil was the worst consequence because they denied the signs of Allah and used to ridicule them."

ثُمَّ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَـٰٓـُٔوا۟ ٱلسُّوٓأَىٰٓ – the outcome for those who engaged in the worst forms of evil was السُّوأَى, the absolute worst consequence. The word أَسَاءُوا is an emphasized form meaning they committed terrible evil. Their ending matched their deeds.

What was their specific crime? أَن كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ – they denied the ayahs of Allah ﷻ, they rejected His revelations, they called His signs lies.

وَكَانُوا۟ بِهَا يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ – and they used to mock them, to ridicule them, to make fun of Allah's ﷻ revelations. This is the worst form of evil – not just disbelief, but active mockery of divine guidance. Taking what Allah ﷻ revealed and treating it as a joke.

translation: Allah begins creation; then He will repeat it; then to Him you will be returned
commentary:
ٱللَّهُ يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ

"Allah begins creation; then He will repeat it; then to Him you will be returned."

ٱللَّهُ يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ – Allah ﷻ is the One Who starts creation off, Who gives creation its beginning. Look at how the previous discussion asked: haven't you looked at how creation started? Haven't you traveled the earth and seen the ruins of past civilizations? Now Allah ﷻ declares explicitly: I am the One Who began it all.

ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ – then He brings it back. He will recreate you. The One Who created you the first time will create you again for the Day of Resurrection.

ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ – then to Him alone you will all be returned. Your final destination is Allah ﷻ. Every soul will stand before Him.

translation: And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will be in despair
commentary:
وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يُبْلِسُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ

"And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will be in despair."

وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ – the day when the Hour rises, when the Hour stands, when the Hour is established. The Hour – الساعة – is one of the many names for Judgment Day.

يُبْلِسُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ – the criminals will be يُبْلِس, utterly hopeless, completely despairing. The word إِبْلِيس – the name of Satan – comes from the same root, meaning one who has lost all hope of Allah's ﷻ mercy. On that Day, the criminals will experience total despair when they realize there's no escape from what awaits them.

translation: And there will not be for them among their [alleged] partners any intercessors, and they will [then] be disbelievers in their partners
commentary:
وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُم مِّن شُرَكَآئِهِمْ شُفَعَـٰٓؤُا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ كَـٰفِرِينَ

"And there will not be for them among their partners any intercessors, and they will be disbelievers in their partners."

وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُم مِّن شُرَكَآئِهِمْ شُفَعَـٰٓؤُا۟ – they will not find among their so-called partners any شُفَعَاء, any intercessors. The word شَفِيع means someone who stands up for you, who makes your case, who vouches for you. In old English translations, this was rendered as "intercessor" – one who intercedes on your behalf.

Those who worshipped false gods did so thinking these gods would intercede with Allah ﷻ on their behalf. "We worship them so they can bring us closer to Allah," they claimed. But on Judgment Day, those false gods – whether idols, prophets wrongly deified, or other created beings – will not speak up for their worshippers. In fact, they'll distance themselves completely.

وَكَانُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ كَـٰفِرِينَ – and they will be deniers of their partners. This works two ways. First, the false gods themselves will deny ever asking to be worshipped: "I'm just a stone," or "I'm just a human being – I never told you to worship me!" Second, once the criminals realize these false gods can't help them get into Paradise, they'll try to change their story: "No, no, we didn't really worship them!" But it will be too late.

translation: And the Day the Hour appears - that Day they will become separated
commentary:
وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَفَرَّقُونَ

"And the Day the Hour appears – that Day they will become separated."

وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَفَرَّقُونَ – on that Day when the Hour is established, they will be يَتَفَرَّقُونَ, completely separated, divided into distinct groups. The word تَفَرُّق means to be cut apart from each other. Families will be separated. Friends will be separated. Communities will be divided.

The word also carries connotations of hostility and competition. "I'm a different faction from you. I don't want anything to do with you. In fact, I'm against you now." All the bonds that held people together in this world will be severed, and people will turn against each other, desperate to save themselves.

translation: And as for those who had believed and done righteous deeds, they will be in a garden [of Paradise], delighted
commentary:
فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَهُمْ فِى رَوْضَةٍ يُحْبَرُونَ

"And as for those who believed and did righteous deeds, they will be in a garden, delighted."

فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ – as for those who believed and did righteous deeds, here's their fate:

فَهُمْ فِى رَوْضَةٍ – they will be in a رَوْضَة. A rawdah is a garden so lush and colorful that you can see the effect of rain in its texture and vibrancy. In Arabic, when they say الأرض أَرَاضَت, it means the rain gave life to the earth and made it bursting with color. A rawdah is a garden full of visual delight – so many colors and textures that your senses are completely stimulated just walking through it.

يُحْبَرُونَ – they will be filled with حُبُور, pure joy and happiness. The word comes from حِبْر, which means ink, but specifically beautiful, decorative ink used to embellish writing. So يُحْبَرُونَ means to be filled with beauty, to be adorned with joy and pleasantness. They're not just happy – they're in a state of aesthetic and spiritual bliss.

translation: But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our verses and the meeting of the Hereafter, those will be brought into the punishment [to remain]
commentary:
وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَلِقَآئِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ

"But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter, those will be brought into the punishment."

وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا – and as for those who disbelieved and lied about Our miraculous signs

وَلِقَآئِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ – and about the meeting of the Hereafter, the meeting of that final Day

فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ – those will be مُحْضَرُون in the punishment. This word means to be formally brought, to be summoned, to be presented. It's the language of being called into court.

Imagine: the principal calls you into his office and you're standing there. The HR department wants to speak to you in their office. A soldier is summoned to the general's quarters for a court martial. You have a court date and you're standing before the judge about to be questioned. You've been brought there – مُحْضَر – to explain yourself, to be scolded, to be judged. That's the state of the disbelievers on Judgment Day. One group is relaxing in a garden; the other is being hauled in to answer for their crimes.

translation: So exalted is Allah when you reach the evening and when you reach the morning
commentary:
فَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ حِينَ تُمْسُونَ وَحِينَ تُصْبِحُونَ

"So exalt Allah when you reach the evening and when you reach the morning."

Given that you know you're going to be standing before Allah ﷻ, given that you know a beautiful garden awaits those who believe and a terrible reckoning awaits those who reject – what should you do?

فَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ – declare the perfection of Allah ﷻ. It's critical to understand that سُبْحَانَ ٱللَّهِ is not a statement of fact like الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ or اللهُ أَكْبَر. Those are statements of reality: "All praise belongs to Allah," "Allah is Greater." But سُبْحَانَ ٱللَّهِ is a call to action. It's actually a shortened form of an implied verb: أُسَبِّحُ سُبْحَانَ ٱللَّهِ – "I declare the perfection of Allah."

The presence of a verbal noun (مَصْدَر) implies action. When you say SubhanAllah, you're making a choice. You're declaring that Allah ﷻ is above and beyond all imperfection, all limitation, all negative attribution. This is the fundamental starting point: declare His perfection.

حِينَ تُمْسُونَ وَحِينَ تُصْبِحُونَ – when you're spending your evenings and when you're spending your mornings. These are active times. Most people are busy in the morning getting ready for their day and busy in the evening coming home from work. Yet Allah ﷻ says: even in these busy times, declare My perfection.

translation: And to Him is [due all] praise throughout the heavens and the earth. And [exalted is He] at night and when you are at noon
commentary:
وَلَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَعَشِيًّا وَحِينَ تُظْهِرُونَ

"And to Him is due all praise throughout the heavens and the earth. And in the late afternoon and when you are at midday."

Now comes the statement of fact: وَلَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ – and to Him alone belongs all الحَمْد, all praise, all gratitude, in the heavens and the earth. This is reality. Whether you acknowledge it or not, whether you declare His perfection or not, hamd already belongs to Him.

وَعَشِيًّا – and in عَشِيّ, the late night, the time when people are sleeping. This is downtime. Most people aren't engaged in remembering Allah ﷻ when they're asleep.

وَحِينَ تُظْهِرُونَ – and when you are تُظْهِرُونَ, in the middle of the afternoon, around the time of Dhuhr prayer. For many people, this is midday break time, siesta time, or work time. These are not typical times for worship or remembrance.

Yet Allah ﷻ says: even in your downtime, even when you're busy working, even when you're taking your afternoon nap, hamd still belongs to Me. The contrast is beautiful: morning and evening (mentioned in the previous ayah) are active times – SubhanAllah, a call to action. Late night and midday are less active times – and hamd belongs to Him anyway, whether you're acknowledging it or not.

The lesson? Remembrance of Allah ﷻ shouldn't be confined to "convenient" times. Make time for His remembrance even when – especially when – you're busy.

translation: He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living and brings to life the earth after its lifelessness. And thus will you be brought out
commentary:
يُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتَ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ وَيُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ

"He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living and gives life to the earth after its lifelessness. And thus you will be brought out."

يُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ – He extracts the living from the dead. A seed appears dead, yet it produces living plants. An egg seems lifeless, yet a chick emerges. Parents might be spiritually dead, yet a righteous child is born who brings life wherever they go.

وَيُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتَ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ – and He brings the dead out of the living. A living tree produces fruit that eventually dies. Living parents might have children who become spiritually dead.

وَيُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا – and if you want to see a clear example of this, He gives life to the earth after it had died. Barren, cracked earth that seems completely lifeless – send rain upon it and suddenly it explodes with vegetation, with color, with life.

وَكَذَٰلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ – and it is just like that, in exactly that manner, that you will all be brought out. You will be resurrected from death just as easily as dead earth springs to life with rain. This is not difficult for Allah ﷻ. He does it every season. You see it with your own eyes.

The Surah began with talk of Rome and Persia, these great empires at war. But Allah ﷻ is teaching us: don't just focus on world events and news headlines. That's just surface-level observation. These miraculous signs are everywhere around you. Nations rise and fall, but life and death happen every single day in countless ways. Allah ﷻ creates life and death constantly. The rise and fall of empires is nothing compared to the signs of life and death He shows you daily.

translation: And of His signs is that He created you from dust; then, suddenly you were human beings dispersing [throughout the earth]
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَنتُم بَشَرٌ تَنتَشِرُونَ

"And of His signs is that He created you from dust; then suddenly you were human beings dispersing throughout the earth."

Now Allah ﷻ begins a beautiful series of ayahs, each starting with وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ  – "and out of His miraculous signs..."

أَنْ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ – He created you from تُرَاب, from dust, from dirt. No matter how great the Roman Empire was, no matter how awesome the Persian Empire was, to Allah ﷻ it's just a bunch of dirt walking around. You are human beings, بَشَر, and we're called bashar because the word comes from بَشَرَة, meaning skin. Unlike animals covered in fur or hair, our skin shows. That's why we're called bashar – we're the creatures of visible skin.

ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَنتُم بَشَرٌ تَنتَشِرُونَ – then suddenly you are human beings spreading everywhere, dispersing, running all over the place. Allah ﷻ just stitched together some dirt, and now you're running around the earth thinking you're so important. This is the origin of human greatness: dirt.

translation: And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا لِّتَسْكُنُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهَا وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُم مَّوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ

"And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought."

This is the famous "wedding card ayah" that every Muslim couple has at their reception. But it's far more significant than we often realize.

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا – and out of His miraculous signs is that He created for you from your own selves أَزْوَاج, spouses, mates. The phrase مِنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ can mean several things: from your own kind (human beings), from your own people, your own community, your extended family. It also alludes to the fact that the first human spouse – Hawwa (AS) – was created from Adam (AS) himself.

Why did Allah ﷻ give us spouses? لِّتَسْكُنُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهَا – so that you may find سَكِينَة, tranquility, peace, calm in them. The word سَكَنَ literally means to stop, to cease movement. When something is disturbed and agitated, it's in a state of حَرَكَة, movement. When it becomes still and peaceful, it's in a state of سُكُون.

Notice the context. This Surah began with war – conflict between Rome and Persia. Two nations engaged in violence and bloodshed. Then Allah ﷻ talks about two human beings coming together, and the purpose of their union is peace. 

The implications are staggering. All of humanity extends from male and female coming together. The act of two human beings forming a bond creates entire nations and civilizations. If the very foundational unit – the married couple – is established on peace, that peace extends to the family, then to the community, then to the nation, and then between nations. But when you don't have peace at this foundational level, that chaos and conflict also expands outward.

Cities riddled with crime are also cities with broken homes, absent fathers, mothers who can't fulfill their roles, parents fighting constantly, abuse and addiction in households. Children raised in such chaos don't value human life. If a child has seen his father beat his mother, why would he feel bad about mugging someone? The lack of peace in the home creates societal breakdown.

This is why marriage is not just a personal matter – it has implications for all of humanity. The spousal relationship is where human civilization begins and either thrives or falls apart.

لِّتَسْكُنُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهَا – so you can find tranquility in her, or in them. The pronoun could refer to both husband finding peace in wife and wife finding peace in husband. The wife says, "I couldn't sleep until you came home. Now I can sleep." The husband had a terrible day, and he just needs his wife to sit next to him for five minutes. Nothing elaborate – just her presence. Just sitting together. That's سَكِينَة. That downtime where you're not talking about anything important, you're just together, and the stress of the day melts away.

Even just lying down next to your spouse at night brings a kind of peace to your heart. You might be exhausted, kids might be jumping all over you, but there's tranquility in that moment. Compare this to sleeping alone on the couch after working late – you wake up the next morning and everything feels off. You're going to have a rough day. She's going to have a rough day. That simple act of being together at night, that shared rest, sets the tone for peace the next day.

When you have peace, it emanates from you. Peace is like light – when you possess it, it radiates outward. You bring peace to the workplace, to your children, to your coworkers, to the masjid. But when you don't have peace inside, you have nothing to give. When there's chaos within you, only chaos comes out. That's why good marriages are so essential for peace in society.

وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُم مَّوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً – and He placed between you مَوَدَّة and رَحْمَة. 

Mawaddah is intense love, passionate love. It comes from وَدّ, which means to want something desperately, to desire to be with someone, to long for them. In the middle of the day, you send a random text: "I miss you." Just out of nowhere, you turn to your spouse and say, "I love you so much." That obsessive, beautiful love – where does it come from? Allah ﷻ put it there. When people first get married, they feel this overwhelming love they've never experienced before, and they wonder, "What's happening to me?"

And here's something fascinating: newly married couples often find themselves randomly falling asleep together. They're just sitting together, and suddenly they're both out. There's this profound peace, this mental clarity they never had before. The husband says to his wife, "I feel like I can think clearly for the first time in my life. There's so much peace. What is this?" And she responds, "It's us."

But then life intrudes. The honeymoon ends. Work starts again, school resumes, families get involved. His family, her family, the in-laws, the siblings. Comments are made. "Didn't I tell you to do that?" "You forgot!" "Well, didn't I tell you not to tell me what to do?" Things can get ugly.

That's why Allah ﷻ didn't just put mawaddah between spouses – He also put rahmah. We've discussed rahmah extensively. It's that special bond of love that makes you want to protect someone, to care for them, to ensure their needs are met. When you're truly married, you stop thinking of yourself as an individual. You're about to buy food and you automatically think, "Wait, I need to get two of those." You don't even consciously think about it – it's automatic. You don't buy one toothbrush anymore. You can't. You're constantly thinking of the other person.

This is mercy in marriage. Later in marriage, mercy becomes even more critical than initial passionate love. That special protective love – comparable to the bond between mother and child – is the bond Allah ﷻ creates between husband and wife.

If marriage creates this level of love, mercy, and protection in a person's life, and they enjoy it themselves, they naturally want others to have it too. That mercy will overflow to others. This is why the ayah ends: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ – indeed, in that are so many miraculous signs for people who really deeply think.

Notice Allah ﷻ didn't say there's "one sign" in marriage – He said آيَات, signs in the plural. Marriage contains countless miraculous signs for those who reflect.

translation: And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your languages and your colors. Indeed in that are signs for those of knowledge
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ خَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوَٰنِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّلْعَـٰلِمِينَ

"And of His signs is the creation of the heavens and earth and the diversity of your languages and your colors. Indeed in that are signs for those of knowledge."

Now that you have tranquility in your home, you don't look at people who speak different languages or have different skin colors as strange or inferior. You see them as signs of Allah ﷻ.

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ خَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ – out of His miraculous signs are the creation of the heavens and the earth

وَٱخْتِلَـٰفُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ – and the differences in your tongues, your languages

وَأَلْوَٰنِكُمْ – and your colors, your skin tones, your physical features

Allah ﷻ calls different languages آيَات, miraculous signs. He calls different races and skin colors آيَات. When someone makes fun of another race, they're making fun of an ayah of Allah ﷻ. When someone mocks another nation's language, they're mocking a sign of Allah ﷻ. How can someone be racist? How can someone hate another nation when their very existence is from His miraculous signs?

Think about it: Allah ﷻ created Adam and Hawwa (AS). They must have had some skin color, some set of features. In His wisdom, He could have made all of us look exactly like them – no variation in skin color, no differences in features. After all, He created us from one source, from one type of dust. Yet from that single origin, He created all this diversity in how we look, in the texture of our "dirt," so to speak.

He made this happen purposefully. Had He wanted, He could have made us one nation, one people, all identical. But this diversity exists for those who want to know Him.

إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّلْعَـٰلِمِينَ – in that are miraculous signs for those who seek knowledge, for العَالِمِين, those who want to know.

translation: And of His signs is your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty. Indeed in that are signs for a people who listen
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ مَنَامُكُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَٱبْتِغَآؤُكُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ

"And of His signs is your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty. Indeed in that are signs for a people who listen."

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ مَنَامُكُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ – out of His miraculous signs is your ability to sleep and dream at night, and sometimes during the day

وَٱبْتِغَآؤُكُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ – and your seeking out of His bounty, going out to work and earn a living

Your ability to sleep at night and get proper rest, and as a result, your ability to wake up on time, get dressed, get in the car, navigate traffic, arrive at work, and actually be productive – all of this is from the miraculous signs of Allah ﷻ. He takes credit for all of it.

This transforms everything into a spiritual experience. Imagine: you're in the middle of New York City. You left your house at 7 AM and stopped at a bus stop where 20 other people are waiting. You squeeze onto a crowded bus where there's barely room to stand. You transfer to the subway. You emerge onto the street and there's an army of people on the sidewalk heading to work – so many you can't even see the next block.

Every single one of those people was asleep at some point – that was from Allah ﷻ. Every single one of them, Allah ﷻ woke up. Every single one of them, He enabled to get to work. Every human being in that morning commute is an ayah of Allah ﷻ.

When you're stuck in traffic feeling frustrated, remember: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ – in that are signs for people who listen, for those who truly want to hear what He's saying. Suddenly, the ayahs come to life all around you. This is spirituality in Islam: seeing the world and being reminded of an ayah, reading an ayah and being reminded of the world, and both reminding you of Allah ﷻ.

translation: And of His signs is [that] He shows you the lightning [causing] fear and aspiration, and He sends down rain from the sky by which He brings to life the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed in that are signs for a people who use reason
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ يُرِيكُمُ ٱلْبَرْقَ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَيُحْىِۦ بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ

"And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning causing fear and aspiration, and He sends down rain from the sky, giving life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed in that are signs for a people who use reason."

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ يُرِيكُمُ ٱلْبَرْقَ – out of His signs is that He shows you lightning

خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا – creating both خَوْف (fear) and طَمَع (hope, aspiration). When people see lightning, there's an immediate reaction: "Oh my God, a storm!" But immediately after: "Yes! Rain is coming!" The same phenomenon creates fear and hope simultaneously.

This is exactly like revelation. Revelation also comes from the sky, and it too shows us ayahs that both frighten us and give us hope.

وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً – and He sends down water from the sky

فَيُحْىِۦ بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ – giving life to the earth after it had died

Look at the sequence carefully: first comes lightning (warning and good news), then comes water from the sky (divine aid), then the earth springs back to life. 

Now apply this to the seerah of the Prophet ﷺ: In Makkah, the Quran came down – that was the lightning, full of warning and hope. Then Allah ﷻ sent His special aid in Madinah – that was the rain. Eventually, the entire region was overtaken by Islam – that was the dead earth coming back to life. When you see lightning, then rain, then greenery, you're witnessing a reenactment of the Prophet's ﷺ life and mission.

إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ – in that are miraculous signs for people who want to use their عَقْل (intellect), who want to apply their minds and reason through what they're seeing.

translation: And of His signs is that the heaven and earth remain by His command. Then when He calls you with a [single] call from the earth, immediately you will come forth
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَن تَقُومَ ٱلسَّمَآءُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ ۚ ثُمَّ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ إِذَآ أَنتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ

"And of His signs is that the heaven and earth stand firm by His command. Then when He calls you with a single call from the earth, immediately you will come forth."

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَن تَقُومَ ٱلسَّمَآءُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ – from His miraculous signs is that the sky and the earth stand firm, are maintained, continue to exist by His command, by His decision alone. He's holding them in place. He allows them to continue.

ثُمَّ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ – then one day, suddenly, He calls you with one single call from the earth

Think about the adhan. When the muadhin calls الله أكبر, حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاة– "Come to prayer! Hurry to success!" – how many people actually get up and move? Whether it's on your phone or broadcast from the masjid's speakers, whether you're in the Muslim world where you hear it echoing through the streets – most people don't respond. They keep walking, keep working, keep doing whatever they're doing.

But on Judgment Day, Allah ﷻ will make one call – just one. إِذَآ أَنتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ – and immediately, without hesitation, every single human being who ever lived will rise from the earth and come forth. One call, and the entirety of humanity responds instantly.

translation: And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and earth. All are to Him devoutly obedient
commentary:
وَلَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ كُلٌّ لَّهُۥ قَـٰنِتُونَ

"And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are to Him devoutly obedient."

وَلَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ – and He owns whoever is in the heavens and the earth

كُلٌّ لَّهُۥ قَـٰنِتُونَ – every single one of them is قَانِت to Him. The word قُنُوت means to be in a state of devout obedience, to be subservient, to be in complete submission.

This has two meanings. First, in the context of Judgment Day: on that Day, everyone will be eagerly, enthusiastically obedient to Allah ﷻ. They'll be like the over-enthusiastic student asking, "What's the homework? What's the homework?" desperately wanting to obey, desperately seeking any opportunity to please Him. They'll be completely submitted, anticipating His next command so they can rush to fulfill it.

But the second meaning applies right now: our bodies, our limbs – all of them are already in complete submission to Allah ﷻ. And on Judgment Day, they're going to testify. Your hands will say, "Ya Allah, You gave this person's heart control over me, and he used me against Your will. I have a complaint to launch against him." Your feet, your tongue, your eyes – all of them will speak up and distance themselves from your choices.

translation: And it is He who begins creation; then He repeats it, and that is [even] easier for Him. To Him belongs the highest attribute in the heavens and earth. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise
commentary:
وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ وَهُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ ۚ وَلَهُ ٱلْمَثَلُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ

"And it is He who begins creation; then He repeats it, and that is even easier for Him. To Him belongs the highest attribute in the heavens and earth. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise."

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ – He is the One Who begins creation, then He will bring it back, repeat it

وَهُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ – and that is even أَهْوَن (ahwan) for Him, easier for Him

This is a critical argument in the Quran based on universal human experience. The Quran doesn't rely primarily on abstract philosophical arguments – it uses experiential reasoning, things everyone can relate to.

Think about it: the first time you had to give a speech, was it easier or harder than the second time? Obviously harder the first time. You start a new job and your boss assigns you a task. The first time you do it is difficult. The second, third, fourth time? It gets easier and easier. Anything you create or do for the first time is harder; repeating it is always easier. This is universal human experience.

If I'm preparing a detailed lecture, the first time I teach it, I'm still working through it in my head. The second time I teach it, I do a much better job. By the third, fourth, fifth time, I'm on autopilot. It's like riding a bike – the file just plays automatically. I've even memorized the jokes. Students who've heard them before say, "We already heard that one," but I can't help it. It's pre-programmed.

Allah ﷻ is using our own experience to answer our doubts. Your objection is: how can He create us again? How can He resurrect us? But your own life experience tells you that whenever you do something, doing it again is easier than the first time. Allah ﷻ already created you once. Isn't it logical, based on your own experience, that creating you a second time would be even easier for Him?

وَلَهُ ٱلْمَثَلُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ – and to Him belongs المَثَل الأَعْلَى, the highest example, the most supreme description in the heavens and earth.

This phrase has layers of meaning. When we talk about Allah ﷻ and want to help someone understand something about Him, Allah ﷻ Himself sometimes uses examples. But He uses the highest, most supreme examples. More broadly, this means Allah ﷻ alone possesses the ability to give the perfect example for anything. He is the ultimate communicator.

Notice what comes next: the following ayah is itself an example – Allah ﷻ is about to demonstrate His ability to give the most fitting, relatable example.

وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ – and He is Al-Aziz, the Exalted in Might, the Ultimate Authority, and Al-Hakim, the Perfectly Wise in how He creates and communicates.

translation: He presents to you an example from yourselves. Do you have among those whom your right hands possess any partners in what We have provided for you so that you are equal therein [and] would fear them as your fear of one another [within a partnership]? Thus do We detail the verses for a people who use reason
commentary: ضَرَبَ لَكُم مَّثَلًا مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ ۖ هَل لَّك

translation: But those who wrong follow their [own] desires without knowledge. Then who can guide one whom Allah has sent astray? And for them there are no helpers

So direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. [Adhere to] the fitrah of Allah upon which He has created [all] people. No change should there be in the creation of Allah. That is the correct religion, but most of the people do not know
[Adhere to it], turning in repentance to Him, and fear Him and establish prayer and do not be of those who associate others with Allah
[Or] of those who have divided their religion and become sects, every faction rejoicing in what it has
commentary:
مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ وَكَانُوا۟ شِيَعًا ۖ كُلُّ حِزْبٍۭ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ

This ayah speaks of those who broke their religion apart—those who divided what was meant to be unified. The word دِينَ connects back to the previous ayah, which established الدِّينِ الْقَيِّمِ, the upright religion. We discussed earlier how the foundations of this deen are embedded within human nature itself, within the fitrah. Yet despite this natural foundation, people manipulated the actual revelation and fragmented it into many different religions.

The profound reality Islam presents is that all religions are actually offshoots of the original Islam. Every religion we find in the world today began with the same pure message, rooted in the fitrah of the human being. But through corruption—through additions and alterations piled on over time—they took the twisted forms we see today.

وَكَانُوا۟ شِيَعًا (wa kaanu shiya'an) - they became factions. The word شِيَعًا indicates groups that are aggressive toward others, exclusive in their identity. كُلُّ حِزْبٍۭ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ - every single group is extremely happy with whatever they have, deeply content with their own worldview, showing off their religious doctrine. The word فَرِحُونَ captures this sense of pride and exclusivity—they want to go out of their way to demonstrate that "we are different from those people, and those people, and those people. Our denomination is unique because of these specific reasons."

Within Islam itself, we witness this phenomenon. Groups emerge claiming to be the only ones with true guidance while everyone else remains lost. This manifests in different ways—some communities develop what amounts to a uniform in dress, others elevate certain literature to near-sacred status, creating exclusive circles around particular books rather than the Quran itself. Sometimes it takes the form of specific practices, or a particular appearance. The sectarian mindset can reach such extremes that people stop seeing other Muslims as genuine members of the Ummah, viewing them instead as lost causes headed for Hellfire simply because they don't belong to a particular group or don't follow specific methodologies.

translation: And when adversity touches the people, they call upon their Lord, turning in repentance to Him. Then when He lets them taste mercy from Him, at once a party of them associate others with their Lord
commentary:
وَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلنَّاسَ ضُرٌّ دَعَوْا۟ رَبَّهُم مُّنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَذَاقَهُم مِّنْهُ رَحْمَةً إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ

When harm touches people—when trouble truly hits them—all the religions, all the factions, all that ideological discourse simply disappears. People just turn to Allah ﷻ in their times of trouble: "Ya Allah, help me." The sectarian divisions evaporate in moments of genuine crisis.

ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَذَاقَهُم مِّنْهُ رَحْمَةً - then when He makes them taste some mercy from Him, some relief from their difficulty, إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ - all of a sudden a group among them starts committing shirk once again with their Master. They return to their associations and false worship.

translation: So that they will deny what We have granted them. Then enjoy yourselves, for you are going to know
commentary:
لِيَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ ۚ فَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ

This ayah continues the theme from the previous surah. لِيَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ - so that they may continue to disbelieve in whatever good We had provided to them. Allah ﷻ gives them another chance, allowing them to show their true nature—that they are not stable in their religion, only turning to Him in emergencies while having no real need for God otherwise.

Notice the subtle grammatical shift here. The ayah begins in third person: "so that they should disbelieve in what We have given them." But then it suddenly shifts to second person: فَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ - "so you enjoy yourselves, you will soon find out."

This transition from "they" to "you" serves as a powerful rhetorical device. Imagine a teacher discussing a student in third person: "There's a student who thinks he can get away with cheating, who thinks he's dealing with someone who doesn't have access to the Internet." The student might not even be paying attention—after all, it's just "he" being discussed. But then suddenly: "Hey, you, Kareem, come here." The shift from third person to direct address jolts the listener, creating a moment of fear and accountability. Allah ﷻ employs this same technique: speaking about them in third person, then suddenly turning the attention directly on the Quraysh themselves: "You better enjoy yourselves. You will soon find out."

translation: Or have We sent down to them an authority, and it speaks of what they were associating with Him
commentary:
أَمْ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَـٰنًا فَهُوَ يَتَكَلَّمُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يُشْرِكُونَ

Is it the case that We sent down upon them some kind of authoritative proof, some kind of validation, فَهُوَ يَتَكَلَّمُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يُشْرِكُونَ - and this authoritative proof speaks on behalf of the shirk they commit? What document do they possess from Allah ﷻ that allows them to fabricate their religion?

translation: And when We let the people taste mercy, they rejoice therein, but if evil afflicts them for what their hands have put forth, immediately they despair
commentary:
وَإِذَآ أَذَقْنَا ٱلنَّاسَ رَحْمَةً فَرِحُوا۟ بِهَا ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌۢ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ إِذَا هُمْ يَقْنَطُونَ

When We make human beings taste any mercy—when things work out, when you get into the school you wanted, when you receive the grades you hoped for, when you land the job you applied for—فَرِحُوا۟ بِهَا, they become extremely happy, overjoyed. Some form of mercy shows from Allah ﷻ and people get overwhelmingly happy.

وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌۢ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ - but if any calamity, any harm, any difficulty hits them because of what their own hands have earned, إِذَا هُمْ يَقْنَطُونَ - all of a sudden these same people lose all hope. They're moping around, not talking to anyone. "What happened?" "I don't want to talk about it." Complete despair sets in. They didn't make the team, they received a bad grade, they didn't get accepted—suddenly they're completely depressed, having lost all hope.

Notice the beautiful balance in how Allah ﷻ phrases this. When discussing mercy, He says وَإِذَآ أَذَقْنَا ٱلنَّاسَ رَحْمَةً - "when We make them taste mercy," explicitly mentioning Himself as the source. But when discussing hard times, He doesn't say "when We make them taste hardship." Instead, He removes Himself from the statement: وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌۢ - "when harm hits them," without mentioning Himself. He associates Himself directly with mercy while distancing Himself grammatically from calamity, though both ultimately come by His will.

translation: Do they not see that Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]? Indeed, in that are signs for a people who believe
commentary:
أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ

Haven't they observed that Allah ﷻ expands provision for whoever He wants, and He limits it, setting restraints on it when He wants? إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ - in that, no doubt, there are miraculous signs for a nation that truly wants to believe.

translation: So give the relative his right, as well as the needy and the traveler. That is best for those who desire the countenance of Allah, and it is they who will be the successful
commentary:
فَـَٔاتِ ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ حَقَّهُۥ وَٱلْمِسْكِينَ وَٱبْنَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ

Allah ﷻ gives to some and withholds from others. But if He has given you wealth, what should you do with it? فَـَٔاتِ ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ حَقَّهُۥ - then give to those possessing closeness to you their right. Notice Allah ﷻ doesn't say "give them some of your money" or "give them from your wealth." He says حَقَّهُۥ - give them their right. He has made that wealth a right belonging to certain people around you.

ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ can refer to those connected by blood—family relations—but it extends beyond biological ties. It includes your closest circle: your immediate family, then cousins, uncles, and aunts, but also your closest friends, your next-door neighbor who you know well. The concept starts with family and expands outward to encompass your immediate circle of acquaintances. If you have a widowed aunt or an orphaned cousin, their need becomes your responsibility. The Prophet ﷺ taught us الْخَالَةُ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْأُمِّ - the maternal aunt holds the status of one's mother.

وَٱلْمِسْكِينَ - and the one stuck in economic difficulty, someone who cannot move out of their problem, who cannot help themselves. وَٱبْنَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ - and someone in the middle of a journey, someone on the road. Travelers don't have the comfort of home, so we must try to help them. If you're traveling with family and stop at a cousin's house, or relatives stop at yours with their children, they'll need baby food, dinner, a comfortable place to sleep—these things become necessary because they're displaced from home.

ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ - that is better for those who seek the face of Allah ﷻ. Notice the phrase "seeking the face of Allah" appeared earlier in the surah, and continues as a central theme here. وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ - and those, in fact, are the ones who are ultimately successful.

translation: And whatever you give for interest to increase within the wealth of people will not increase with Allah. But what you give in zakah, desiring the countenance of Allah - those are the multipliers
commentary:
وَمَآ ءَاتَيْتُم مِّن رِّبًا لِّيَرْبُوَا۟ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِ ٱلنَّاسِ فَلَا يَرْبُوا۟ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَآ ءَاتَيْتُم مِّن زَكَوٰةٍ تُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُضْعِفُونَ

The basic philosophy behind riba in Islam addresses two fundamental ethical problems that come together in such transactions. First, it creates a way of making money that's guaranteed—there's no risk involved. Second, it doesn't require any work on your part. Money simply sits and grows on its own. When these two elements combine in an investment, we encounter the core ethical violation of riba.

Consider different scenarios. If you invest in land—you buy a property or a patch of ground—prices might rise or fall. There's no guarantee the value will increase. This isn't riba because risk exists. But when you place money in a savings account with a guaranteed 2% return that accumulates year after year, this enters questionable territory. You didn't do anything to earn those returns, and the growth is assured.

Riba also means demanding more than what something is worth, or charging extra on top of what is owed. If you owe me $10 but will pay next month, and I respond, "Okay, but if you're paying next month, make it $12," I've earned an extra $2 without any investment on my part. There was no risk—you signed a paper obligating you to pay $12. Those additional $2 came without any actual effort or risk from me.

The Quran addresses this in greater detail in Surah Al-Baqarah, but its mention here in Makkan revelation—before the detailed legal rulings were revealed—demonstrates its significance. This isn't merely a legal technicality. It's not just another restriction like dietary laws. Certain matters create such devastating consequences in society, such profound injustice, that they demand attention even in Makkan surahs, which primarily address moral and ethical foundations. Riba ranks among Islam's major forms of social injustice.

How does interest create injustice? Consider a simple example. Someone purchased a house in the 1940s for $20,000, but they bought it on a mortgage—a 30-year mortgage they'd be paying until the 1980s. They didn't actually pay $20,000. By the time they finished those mortgage payments, they'd paid perhaps $40,000 for that same house. Now it's time to sell. Will they sell it for $21,000 because the original price was $20,000? Of course not. They invested $40,000, so they'll try to get at least $45,000. The same house now sells for $45,000.

The next buyer takes out a mortgage, paying until 2010. By then, for that same house, they've paid $250,000. When they sell, they'll want at least $170,000. Follow this forward. The same patch of dirt, the exact same land that existed 80 years ago, still the same physical material—yet the price has skyrocketed. Have we not created more poverty? Have we not taken away opportunity? Have we not made home ownership something only the wealthy can access?

As this system continues, people earning moderate incomes cannot even dream of owning a house. If they try, they must accept severely substandard housing just to achieve ownership. This is robbery disguised as finance.

Consider another angle: gold. If you owned a brick of gold in the 1920s and a bank offered you $20,000 cash for it, you made the exchange. That cash sat in a box for three generations. The fourth generation opens the box and finds a note: "Dear great-grandson, this is what the bank gave me for our brick of gold. If you want, you can take this back to the bank and retrieve the gold." Can he walk into a bank with $20,000 and get that brick of gold back? They'd give him a small coin, perhaps a thin slice of the brick. That person was robbed of real material wealth when he accepted cash. Riba creates imaginary value that fluctuates based on whim rather than reality.

There's speculation about potential conflict affecting oil prices—the stock market tanks. Speculation about interest rates dropping—markets rise. The Federal Reserve chairman says nothing, but speculation alone that he might causes dramatic market movements. Just creating a rumor moves everything up and down. This corruption robs people of homes, robs them of wealth, and buries them under endless debt for things not actually worth the burden.

Consider foreclosures. A friend purchased a house in Washington D.C. for $300,000 on a mortgage. He lost his job during an economic downturn. The house value dropped to $180,000. Over time, his mortgage payments would total around $450,000. If he sells the house at its current market value of $180,000, he still owes $300,000—for nothing. He has nothing tangible in his hand except debt. To whom does he owe this? To a bank. For what? For a house that's the same brick, same floors, same cabinets—the same materials. How did it drop in value? This perceived value creates immense oppression and economic suffering.

Beyond this, predatory lending practices target vulnerable people. College freshmen barely out of high school encounter credit card companies offering free candy alongside credit applications. Refinancing offers promise lower monthly payments but dramatically increase total debt. People accept these arrangements because they solve immediate problems, not realizing they've accumulated debt they cannot hope to pay off in their lifetime. An entire culture develops around minimum payments—minimum on credit cards, minimum on mortgages, minimum on car loans. People survive this way, figuring they'll die before making all the payments anyway, leaving the next generation buried in debt. The nation's deficit grows deeper and deeper because this culture of minimum payments and interest has taken root.

لِّيَرْبُوَا۟ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِ ٱلنَّاسِ فَلَا يَرْبُوا۟ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ - whatever you give seeking growth through people's wealth, it doesn't grow with Allah. وَمَآ ءَاتَيْتُم مِّن زَكَوٰةٍ تُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ - but whatever you give as zakah, seeking the face of Allah, فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُضْعِفُونَ - those are the ones who will have their wealth truly multiplied.

The ayah first mentions giving to close relatives, the poor, and travelers—where disposable income should flow. Then it identifies the best use of wealth, followed by the worst use of wealth (riba), and finally mentions at minimum giving zakah. The detailed laws and percentages for zakah were revealed later, but already we're taught this purifies income. تُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ - if you gave seeking the face of Allah ﷻ, those are the ones whose status and rank will be multiplied.

Riba creates such devastating social consequences that Allah ﷻ addresses it even in Makkan revelation, though it's not merely a legal issue. It ranks among Islam's major forms of social injustice. Obscene price hikes represent another form—when demand is high and companies raise prices excessively because everyone needs their product. The pharmaceutical industry exemplifies this: three-cent pills selling for $85. This is riba. When people lose their ability to afford necessities and fall into debt just to pay medical bills—not for luxury cars or nice homes, but simply to continue breathing—that represents ultimate oppression. They impose penalties for late payments, hoping you'll miss deadlines so they can add late fees and send bills to collection agencies.

Allah ﷻ didn't specify exact profit margins—20% or 30%. But He taught us principles of justice. Islam stands between two extremes. We're not purely capitalist, though Islam doesn't oppose capitalism. We're not communist either, where everyone must be equal. Islam encourages venture capitalism, investment, and entrepreneurship—but with moral, social, and ethical responsibility. We need enterprise, but it must serve society responsibly.

Muslims, especially in the West with disposable income, must become socially responsible entrepreneurs. We should create businesses that profit—profit is not evil—but whose very existence creates goodness in society. The goal isn't merely profit; the goal is benefit to society. Yet the business model should be sound enough to generate income without requiring constant funding, even providing funding to others while simultaneously creating good. Young minds must embrace this vision: power today isn't military might or weapons. Power is economic. It rests with those funding campaigns, with conglomerates. We must develop sophisticated understanding and create socially responsible entrepreneurship. Muslims worldwide are already beginning this work and will continue developing it, insha'Allah.

translation: Allah is the one who created you, then provided for you, then will cause you to die, and then will give you life. Are there any of your "partners" who does anything of that? Exalted is He and high above what they associate with Him
commentary:
ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ رَزَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ۖ هَلْ مِن شُرَكَآئِكُم مَّن يَفْعَلُ مِن ذَٰلِكُم مِّن شَىْءٍ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ

Allah ﷻ is the One who created you, then provided for you, then gives you death, then will give you life again. هَلْ مِن شُرَكَآئِكُم مَّن يَفْعَلُ مِن ذَٰلِكُم مِّن شَىْءٍ - is there any among your partners, your associates, those false gods, that can do any of that for you in any way? سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ - declared is His perfection. وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ - how high He is, far above the associations they make.

translation: Corruption has appeared throughout the land and sea by [reason of] what the hands of people have earned so He may let them taste part of [the consequence of] what they have done that perhaps they will return [to righteousness]
commentary:
ظَهَرَ ٱلْفَسَادُ فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِى ٱلنَّاسِ لِيُذِيقَهُم بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ

Corruption emerged in the land and the sea بِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِى ٱلنَّاسِ - because of what people's hands have earned. What are people earning with their hands? In the previous ayah, we discussed riba. People engage in shirk. Allah ﷻ declares these actions will create enormous corruption in land and sea.

Perhaps no generation has witnessed more corruption resulting from blind greed and riba than ours. Is there not intense pollution in our oceans like never before? Is there not severe corruption on land? Even in a powerful, First World nation, citizens complain about landfills in their neighborhoods, about what oil refineries and coal companies have done. People have become sick—literal فَسَادُ, literal corruption in the land. Beyond this, there's war, piracy, and countless other forms of corruption.

لِيُذِيقَهُم بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟ - so that He may make them taste only some of the consequences of what they have done. Allah ﷻ says the corruption we see—the wars, crime, pollution—represents only a small taste of what humanity has earned. If He gave us the full taste of our actions, that would be Judgment Day itself.

لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ - hopefully they will return, they will come back.

translation: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of those before. Most of them were associators [of others with Allah]
commentary:
قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّشْرِكِينَ

Tell them: Travel through the land. Look for yourselves—what was the outcome of those people who engaged in this behavior long before you? كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّشْرِكِينَ - most of them were people of shirk. Allah ﷻ says most were mushrikeen, implying some weren't. But those who weren't didn't do anything about it. They simply sat around and watched it happen, unconcerned. "At least I have bread on my table. Why should I care about what's happening in society?"

translation: So direct your face toward the correct religion before a Day comes from Allah of which there is no repelling. That Day, they will be divided
commentary:
فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ ٱلْقَيِّمِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ يَوْمٌ لَّا مَرَدَّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصَّدَّعُونَ

Turn your face and establish it firmly for the upright religion مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ يَوْمٌ لَّا مَرَدَّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ - before a day comes that cannot be turned back, that cannot be canceled out, that cannot recede. لَا مَرَدَّ means it cannot recede like water receding—it cannot be pushed back from Allah ﷻ. Nobody can make the consequences of that day go away.

يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصَّدَّعُونَ - on that day they will be broken up, shattered like glass broken into pieces, torn apart never to be brought together again.

translation: Whoever disbelieves - upon him is [the consequence of] his disbelief. And whoever does righteousness - they are for themselves preparing
commentary:
مَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُۥ ۖ وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلِأَنفُسِهِمْ يَمْهَدُونَ

How will they be broken up on that day? مَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُۥ - whoever disbelieves, then his disbelief becomes a liability against him. وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلِأَنفُسِهِمْ يَمْهَدُونَ - and whoever did righteous deeds, then for their own selves they are creating a cradle.

The word يَمْهَدُونَ comes from مَهْد, which means cradle—specifically a mother's cradle. They're creating a place for themselves to relax in. The closest contemporary expression might be a retirement plan or safety net—the security people want for their future. Whoever did good deeds is creating a retirement plan for themselves, a place of comfort and rest.

translation: That He may reward those who have believed and done righteous deeds out of His bounty. Indeed, He does not like the disbelievers
commentary:
لِيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

So that He may fully compensate those who believed and did the righteous deeds expected of them—مِن فَضْلِهِۦٓ, out of His favor. Even though people perform good deeds, it's ultimately Allah's favor that He granted them the opportunity to do those deeds. إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ - no doubt He does not love those who disbelieve.

translation: And of His signs is that He sends the winds as bringers of good tidings and to let you taste His mercy and so the ships may sail at His command and so you may seek of His bounty, and perhaps you will be grateful
commentary:
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَن يُرْسِلَ ٱلرِّيَاحَ مُبَشِّرَٰتٍ وَلِيُذِيقَكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَلِتَجْرِىَ ٱلْفُلْكُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ

Among His miraculous signs is that He sends winds that carry powerful good news. Winds themselves bring news—news of approaching rain, news of changing seasons. وَلِيُذِيقَكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ - so that He can make you taste some of His loving mercy.

وَلِتَجْرِىَ ٱلْفُلْكُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ - and so that ships may sail by His command. Those winds enable ships to sail properly by His command. Some might think this no longer applies since modern ships have powerful turbine engines and aren't affected by wind anymore. But consider: most people don't travel by ship anymore—they travel by air. When there's wind turbulence, planes must alter course, pilots must ground aircraft, they monitor wind direction on radar constantly. Ships sailed by His command, and these airships do as well.

وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ - and so that you may pursue out of His favor. The potential for flight always existed, but that we get to witness it, that we experience it—this is from His favor. Sitting on a plane with 300 other people flying through the air—if you pause to think about what's actually happening, it's staggering. Something a crane couldn't even lift is flying through the air and will land so smoothly you'll barely feel a bump. This is mind-boggling.

Human beings think it's our accomplishment—we figured out the technology of flight, air friction, momentum. But we didn't invent momentum or air friction. We discovered them. The treasure was already there. We didn't invent metal, copper for electricity, or electricity itself. These were all there. We didn't make anything; we simply used what was provided. The ingredients for all these technologies were created by Allah ﷻ, and the minds we used to combine them are also from Him.

Remember when Allah ﷻ commanded Nuh (AS) to build the first ship? He provided the architectural design: "I'll tell you where to put each plank, how to create proper buoyancy, what angles to use. With that many people and animals, this is how you make corridors. Put them in this section because if you place them there, the ship might sink." The entire architectural design came from Allah ﷻ. Everything thereafter is a byproduct of that original divine instruction.

وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ - so that all of you may be grateful.

translation: And We have already sent messengers before you to their peoples, and they came to them with clear evidences; then We took retribution from those who committed crimes, and incumbent upon Us was support of the believers
commentary:
وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ ۖ وَكَانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نَصْرُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

We swear that We had already sent, long before you, messengers to their nations. فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ - they came with the clearest proofs. فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ - then We took revenge against those who committed crimes. The messengers didn't seek revenge—Allah ﷻ took revenge.

وَكَانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نَصْرُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ - and aiding the believers had always been especially a right mandated upon Us. We will absolutely aid Our believers.

translation: It is Allah who sends the winds, and they stir the clouds and spread them in the sky however He wills, and He makes them fragments so you see the rain emerge from within them. And when He causes it to fall upon whom He wills of His servants, immediately they rejoice
commentary:
ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَيَبْسُطُهُۥ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ وَيَجْعَلُهُۥ كِسَفًا فَتَرَى ٱلْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ ۖ فَإِذَآ أَصَابَ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ

Allah ﷻ is the One who sends the winds. فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا - then they carry and load the clouds, lifting them like someone lifts dirt when digging in the earth. The winds lift the clouds. فَيَبْسُطُهُۥ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ - then He spreads them out, expands them, stretches them across the sky however He wants.

وَيَجْعَلُهُۥ كِسَفًا - and then He makes them light-bodied, layered upon layer. فَتَرَى ٱلْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ - then you see light rain, drizzle, continuous light rainfall يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ, coming right through the layers.

فَإِذَآ أَصَابَ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦٓ - then all of a sudden it targets—He targets by means of it whoever He wants from among His slaves. Every drop is targeted; each drop has its designated place.

إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ - then all of a sudden they're congratulating one another, rejoicing, dancing in the rain, running around having fun in the light drizzle.

translation: Although they were, before it was sent down upon them - before that, in despair
commentary:
وَإِن كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِم مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَمُبْلِسِينَ

Even if this is the case—that before He sent rain upon them, long before the rain descended on them, they were completely hopeless, لَمُبْلِسِينَ. Rain would never come. Deep sadness had settled over them, especially in societies experiencing drought where entire farmlands face destruction. A profound sadness permeates the people.

Then a child feels a single drop on his face. His face turns toward the sky and he goes wild, running and screaming, "It's going to rain!" The entire village erupts with excitement. Everyone stands outside, some crying tears of joy. This powerful scene is depicted in the Quran.
