translation: So observe the effects of the mercy of Allah - how He gives life to the earth after its lifelessness. Indeed, that [same one] will give life to the dead, and He is over all things competent
commentary:
فَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِ رَحْمَتِ ٱللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

Then look at the remnants, the traces, the clues of Allah's loving mercy—كَيْفَ يُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ, how He brings life to the earth after its death. إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ - no doubt that same One will absolutely give life to the dead. وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ - and He maintains complete control over all things.

translation: But if We should send a [bad] wind and they saw [their crops] turned yellow, they would remain thereafter disbelievers
commentary:
وَلَئِنْ أَرْسَلْنَا رِيحًا فَرَأَوْهُ مُصْفَرًّا لَّظَلُّوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ يَكْفُرُونَ

Had We sent winds against them—فَرَأَوْهُ مُصْفَرًّا, and they looked at them as yellow, carrying heavy wind with storm approaching—لَّظَلُّوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ يَكْفُرُونَ, they would have remained in disbelief after that. They would have remained ungrateful after witnessing even that sign.

translation: So indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs, retreating
commentary:
فَإِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَلَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا وَلَّوْا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ

Earlier Allah ﷻ spoke about the earth coming back to life. Now He addresses dead hearts. فَإِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ - you cannot make the dead listen. وَلَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ - you cannot make the deaf hear the call إِذَا وَلَّوْا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ - especially when they've turned their backs.

translation: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [in submission to Allah]
commentary:
وَمَآ أَنتَ بِهَـٰدِ ٱلْعُمْىِ عَن ضَلَـٰلَتِهِمْ ۖ إِن تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَهُم مُّسْلِمُونَ

And you are not at all going to guide the blind away from their misguidedness. Allah ﷻ told us to observe various signs throughout this surah—observe this, observe that, look at this, examine that. If someone's perspective on life around them doesn't shift, if they get stuck in traffic without recognizing that as a sign showing how Allah ﷻ enables people to pursue His favor, if their outlook remains unchanged, they're still blind.

The surah began with "they know the outward appearance of worldly life." Here Allah ﷻ reveals the inward reality, showing us another perspective. But if someone perceives only the outward and never sees the inward connection to Allah ﷻ, that person remains blind.

إِن تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا - you can only make listen those who believe in Our signs, فَهُم مُّسْلِمُونَ, and those are the ones who submit.

translation: Allah is the one who created you from weakness, then made after weakness strength, then made after strength weakness and white hair. He creates what He wills, and He is the Knowing, the Competent
commentary:
۞ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن ضَعْفٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا وَشَيْبَةً ۚ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْقَدِيرُ

Allah ﷻ is the One who created you from weakness. You were created from weak fluid, from weak bones. Infants must be held with utmost care—their skulls are so soft you're told not to hold the head directly but from behind. Be careful not to support improperly because the neck might suffer injury. Even during delivery, babies' clavicles sometimes break—the easiest bone to fracture. So fragile, so delicate.

ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً - then after that weakness, He made strength. That same child you once held in your hands, that little boy, now stands taller than his mother, taller than his father. He says, "Dad, don't pick up that couch—I've got it." That same child who was once so small you could carry him around has reached the stage of strength.

ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا - but then after that strength comes another time of weakness. Spending the day leaning back, wrinkling. Someone asks if you can move a chair and you pull a muscle lifting it. Your son asks if you want to play basketball: "I can't—one shot and I'm exhausted. They make those balls heavy nowadays, don't they?"

Allah ﷻ makes you weak again. وَشَيْبَةً - and then comes white hair, grayness. Gray hair serves as an alarm of approaching death. Just as a plant withers and loses its color before dying, your hair withers and loses color. What comes next? The plant dies, disappears. You're withering. The first gray hair appears, then the second. People try coloring it, but it keeps happening.

How much we support the hair coloring industry trying to deny aging, unable to accept growing old. But we must accept it, embrace it. You can color your hair, but even the hair on your arms will turn white. Will you keep coloring everything? You must accept this reality.

يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ - He creates whatever He wants. وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْقَدِيرُ - He is all-knowing, all-powerful. He knows every stage of life you pass through, has calculated exactly when you'll turn a certain age, when you'll first feel that strange sensation in your back, when you'll first run out of breath doing activities that once came easily. He used to run like a horse playing basketball; a few years later playing with teenagers, they're still running while he's gasping for air. Allah ﷻ has calculated lung capacity, physical capability, and when death will approach.

translation: And the Day the Hour appears the criminals will swear they had remained but an hour. Thus they were deluded
commentary:
وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يُقْسِمُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ مَا لَبِثُوا۟ غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَانُوا۟ يُؤْفَكُونَ

On the day when the Hour rises, the criminals will swear مَا لَبِثُوا۟ غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ - they didn't remain in worldly life except for one hour. They'll look back at their entire existence and it will feel as though they were in this world for just an hour. كَذَٰلِكَ كَانُوا۟ يُؤْفَكُونَ - that is how they used to be turned away, how they used to turn away.

translation: But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, "You remained the extent of Allah 's decree until the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not used to know
commentary:
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ وَٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْبَعْثِ ۖ فَهَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلْبَعْثِ وَلَـٰكِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ

Those who were given knowledge and faith will say: لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْبَعْثِ - you remained according to the Book of Allah ﷻ, in the decree of Allah ﷻ, until the Day of Resurrection. فَهَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلْبَعْثِ - and this is the Day of being raised. وَلَـٰكِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ - but you were people who didn't know, who didn't care to know.

translation: So that Day, their excuse will not benefit those who wronged, nor will they be asked to appease [Allah]
commentary:
فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا يَنفَعُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ

On that day, those who did wrong—لَّا يَنفَعُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ, their excuse will not benefit them. "Please don't punish me, I have an excuse, I have a reason"—their justification, their rationale won't help. مَعْذِرَة means to have an excuse, like the bird in Surah An-Naml who said "I have important intelligence information." That's ma'dhirah—a presented excuse.

They'll say, "We didn't know." But Allah ﷻ will respond: How could you not know? Messengers were around you. You didn't know because you didn't listen, you didn't want to know, you didn't think it was important. Your excuse holds no weight here.

وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ - and they will not be given relief from exhaustion. يُسْتَعْتَب means to remove disturbance, remove exhaustion, address complaints. They won't be among those whose problems receive attention.

translation: And We have certainly presented to the people in this Qur'an from every [kind of] example. But, [O Muhammad], if you should bring them a sign, the disbelievers will surely say, "You [believers] are but falsifiers
commentary:
وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ ۚ وَلَئِن جِئْتَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ لَّيَقُولَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ

We have certainly struck for all people in this Quran every kind of example, given all forms of examples. وَلَئِن جِئْتَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ - yet even if you were to come to them with a miraculous sign, لَّيَقُولَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ - those who disbelieve will simply turn around and say, "You're just trying to prove us wrong. You're just trying to cancel out our lifestyle, invalidate our way of living."

translation: Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know
commentary:
كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

That is how Allah ﷻ places a seal, a mark—كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ. The word طَبْع also means nature in Arabic, like a seal that's set and won't move, becoming fixed as part of one's nature. عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ - upon the hearts of those who have no knowledge, those who refused to learn.

translation: So be patient. Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And let them not disquiet you who are not certain [in faith]
commentary:
فَٱصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ

The surah concludes with beautiful consolation. In its opening, we learned the Romans had lost. At its conclusion, the Prophet ﷺ receives this counsel: Don't let those without belief make fun of you. Don't let them underestimate your strength or the strength of Muslims. فَٱصْبِرْ - so be patient.

إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ - no doubt, the promise of Allah ﷻ is true, is real.

وَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ - don't allow those without conviction to consider you light, to consider you powerless. You're few in number, you appear weak, and even people like you—the Romans—have lost. Don't let them make you think you're weak. Don't let them lead you to believe you're lightweight, that you amount to nothing compared to the enemy.

This was one of Pharaoh's tactics of oppression—he wanted to keep his people feeling light, preventing them from developing any confidence in themselves. يَسْتَخِفُّهُمْ فَأَطَاعُوهُ - "he kept them light, so they obeyed him."

Don't lose confidence. When others put you down, don't let that diminish your self-assurance. Today we must teach our youth this crucial lesson because Islam faces constant mockery and dismissal. Muslims are portrayed as easy targets. "Why are there so many things wrong with Islam?" Don't travel down that road of doubt and weakness.

إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ - the promise of Allah ﷻ is true. These people lacking conviction—don't give them the right to speak to you this way. Don't let them address you in this manner. لَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ.
